query: How many TOEFL points or what IELTS band do I need for an English-taught programme?

queries: How many TOEFL points or what IELTS band do I need for an English-taught programme?

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text:
- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 94 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 6.5 points
- Cambridge English First Certificate (FCE): result level B2
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): result level B2
- Language Competency Examination at level B2 by a university language centre. The language centre has to be located at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These certificates may not be older than three years at the time of application.

Any of the above mentioned certificates at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

A proof of English language proficiency at level C1 is required for admission to the master programme in Anglophone Literatures and Cultures, the master programme in English Language and Linguistics and English (Teaching Subject) in the master programme Teacher Education.

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text:
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- English as first language ([see FAQ](#c805025)).
- Proof of erudite language: secondary-school leaving certificate, if the certificate was awarded at an institution
  - with English as the language of education
  - in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 110 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 (at least 7.5 per component)
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

A proof of English language proficiency at level C2 is required for the master programme in Translation, if one of the working languages is English.

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text:
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement

  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a completed degree programme,
  - with English language specific courses,
  - to the extent of at least 36 ECTS.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 115 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 or higher
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), especially §§ 63, 64](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text: / - I had English lessons in school. Is this a proof of English language proficiency? Having had English lessons in school is not sufficient as proof of language proficiency. A secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English from a school in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, New Zealand or Canada is an accepted proof, if the level of English proficiency is noted as B2 or higher. If the level is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded. - I have completed my studies in a non-EU/EEA country. Does this count as proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed a degree of at least two years, taught entirely in English, at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada, it is accepted as proof of English language proficiency. - I have a secondary-school leaving certificate from a non-EU/EEA country with the subject English. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. Exception: If you have completed your secondary-school leaving certificate at an educational institution in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom

retrieved.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
retrieved.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |
| --- | --- |
| [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science-bachelor-with-aptitute-test/) | [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |

The language of instruction of these programmes is German and English.

| Degree Programme | Required Qualification |

| [Economics](/en/bachelordiploma-programmes/economics-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |
| [International Legal Studies](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/international-legal-studies-bachelor-with-entrance-exam-procedure/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) & [English B2](/en/admission/english-language-proficiency/#c805007) (admission requirement) |
| [Physics](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/physics-bachelor-with-osa/) | [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/) (& English B2 recommended) |

retrieved.title: Proof of English language proficiency
retrieved.text:
You must provide a proof of English language proficiency to be admitted to the bachelor programmes in International Legal Studies and Mathematical Foundations of Data Science or [English or multilingual master programmes](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/) at the Universitiy of Vienna.

[## Recognised proofs at level B2](#c805007)

[## Recognised proofs at level C1](#c805011)

[## Recognised proofs at level C2](#c805018)

A proof of English language proficiency at level B2 is required for admission to the bachelor programme in [International Legal Studies](/en/entrance-exam/international-legal-studies/) and in [Mathematical Foundations of Data Science](/en/entrance-exam/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/mathematical-foundations-of-data-science/) as well as to multiple master programmes taught in English and multilingual master programmes.

- [Required English proficiency for admission to master programmes taught in foreign languages](/en/degree-programmes/degree-programmes-in-foreign-languages/)

retrieved.title: The ISU study café
retrieved.text:
The project is especially aimed at students of the University of Vienna who have motivation, concentration and/or attention difficulties.

You can register for the dates via the freely accessible ["ISU -Study-Café"-Moodle course](https://moodle.univie.ac.at/course/view.php?id=442689)!
You can register for the Moodle course yourself at any time.

- On site:
  Max. Number of participants: 14 people per date (first come first serve!)
  In the Moodle course you can see how many places are still available per date.
  The dates are always released by the 20th of the previous month.
- Online:
  Unlimited number of participants; via BigBlueButton.

Contact via: [peerpoint.ctl@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,rggtrqkpv0evnBwpkxkg0ce0cv');)
Please enter “ISU Learning Café” in the subject line.

retrieved.text:
Collapse all

#### Test literature

[Aufnahmeverfahren für das Diplomstudium der Rechtswissenschaften an der Universität Wien – Auszug basierend auf „Grundbegriffe der Rechtswissenschaften“ von Franz-Stefan Meissel; Helmut Ofner; Bettina Perthold-Stoitzner; Michaela Windisch-Graetz](https://phaidra.univie.ac.at/detail/o:2046181) (only available in German)

#### Sample exercises and test structure

[Sample exercises and test structure for the diploma programme in Law](https://self-assessment2.pzle.univie.ac.at/start.php?id=preav_rewi) (only available in German)

Collapse all

Open all

#### Ranking

The ranking is determined as follows:

- 400 of the places go to the test participants with the highest overall scores who have registered with German as their A-language
- 262 of the places go to the test participants with the highest overall scores who have registered with a different language as their A-language

After these, participants with descending overall scores follow alternately (according to the zipper merge method) on the ranking list:

- one test participant who registered with a language other than German as A-language,
- and one test participant who registered with German as A-language.

To complete the admission procedure you also have to fulfil the requirements for admission to a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

#### Test literature

[Transkulturelle Kommunikation: Zur Einführung und Vorbereitung auf das Studium](https://phaidra.univie.ac.at/detail/o:1165618) (only available in German)

/

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

  Related Links:

  - [Range of studies: bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/)

reranked.title: Proof of English language proficiency
reranked.text:
- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 94 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 6.5 points
- Cambridge English First Certificate (FCE): result level B2
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): result level B2
- Language Competency Examination at level B2 by a university language centre. The language centre has to be located at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These certificates may not be older than three years at the time of application.

Any of the above mentioned certificates at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

A proof of English language proficiency at level C1 is required for admission to the master programme in Anglophone Literatures and Cultures, the master programme in English Language and Linguistics and English (Teaching Subject) in the master programme Teacher Education.

reranked.title: Proof of English language proficiency
reranked.text:
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- English as first language ([see FAQ](#c805025)).
- Proof of erudite language: secondary-school leaving certificate, if the certificate was awarded at an institution
  - with English as the language of education
  - in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 110 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 (at least 7.5 per component)
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

A proof of English language proficiency at level C2 is required for the master programme in Translation, if one of the working languages is English.

reranked.text: / - I had English lessons in school. Is this a proof of English language proficiency? Having had English lessons in school is not sufficient as proof of language proficiency. A secondary-school leaving certificate with successful graduation exam in the subject English from a school in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, New Zealand or Canada is an accepted proof, if the level of English proficiency is noted as B2 or higher. If the level is not clearly evident in the certificate, a confirmation by the school administration may be demanded. - I have completed my studies in a non-EU/EEA country. Does this count of proof of English language proficiency? No. Only the proofs mentioned above are accepted. reranked.title: Knowledge of Foreign Languages
reranked.text:
In general, the language of instruction of degree programmes at the University of Vienna is [German](/en/admission/german-language-proficiency/).

Some [programmes](/en/degree-programmes/) are taught in another language or in a combination of German and another language. Legal basis for this is the [curriculum of the respective programme](http://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/).

For some degree programme, you need to prove your language proficiency before admission and upload the certificate in the application. This can be done via your previous studies or a [certificate of the respective language](#c147163).

For other degree programmes, the indicated level is a recommendation. Without knowledge of the language the progress of your studies will be delayed. However, the level of knowledge is not checked during admission process.

The following master programmes are taught completely in English.

#### Test literature

[Aufnahmeverfahren für das Diplomstudium der Rechtswissenschaften an der Universität Wien – Auszug basierend auf „Grundbegriffe der Rechtswissenschaften“ von Franz-Stefan Meissel; Helmut Ofner; Bettina Perthold-Stoitzner; Michaela Windisch-Graetz](https://phaidra.univie.ac.at/detail/o:2046181) (only available in German)

#### Sample exercises and test structure

[Sample exercises and test structure for the diploma programme in Law](https://self-assessment2.pzle.univie.ac.at/start.php?id=preav_rewi) (only available in German)

Collapse all

Open all

#### Ranking

The ranking is determined as follows:

- 400 of the places go to the test participants with the highest overall scores who have registered with German as their A-language
- 262 of the places go to the test participants with the highest overall scores who have registered with a different language as their A-language

After these, participants with descending overall scores follow alternately (according to the zipper merge method) on the ranking list:

- one test participant who registered with a language other than German as A-language,
- and one test participant who registered with German as A-language.

To complete the admission procedure you also have to fulfil the requirements for admission to a bachelor programme at the University of Vienna.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text:
/

- Bachelor programme

  The bachelor programmes offered at the University of Vienna comprise 180 ECTS and are designed for a standard duration of three years (exception: Teacher Education Programme of 240 ECTS with a standard duration of four years). The language of instruction of all bachelor programmes is German, which is why German knowledge is required for the admission. Additionally, English knowledge on level B2 is required for the application for admision for the bachelor programme International Law.
  Graduates of an eligible bachelor programme can apply for admission to a master programme.

  Related Links:

  - [Range of studies: bachelor/diploma programmes](/en/degree-programmes/bachelordiploma-programmes/)
  - [Master Access Guide](/en/degree-programmes/master-programmes/master-access-guide/)

  Related entries:
  - [Curriculum](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=29989&cHash=0b86e44c93fdbfb30111b3c5fddfbb27)

- Board examination

  If you have not passed an exam three times, this exam must be taken orally before an examination committee for the fourth and last time. The registration is done at the StudiesServiceCenter (SSC) or the StudiesServiceUnit (SSS).

selected.title: Proof of English language proficiency
selected.text:
- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 94 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 6.5 points
- Cambridge English First Certificate (FCE): result level B2
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): result level B2
- Language Competency Examination at level B2 by a university language centre. The language centre has to be located at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These certificates may not be older than three years at the time of application.

Any of the above mentioned certificates at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

A proof of English language proficiency at level C1 is required for admission to the master programme in Anglophone Literatures and Cultures, the master programme in English Language and Linguistics and English (Teaching Subject) in the master programme Teacher Education.

selected.title: Proof of English language proficiency
selected.text:
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- English as first language ([see FAQ](#c805025)).
- Proof of erudite language: secondary-school leaving certificate, if the certificate was awarded at an institution
  - with English as the language of education
  - in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 110 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 (at least 7.5 per component)
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

A proof of English language proficiency at level C2 is required for the master programme in Translation, if one of the working languages is English.

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Proof of English language proficiency
- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 94 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 6.5 points
- Cambridge English First Certificate (FCE): result level B2
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): result level B2
- Language Competency Examination at level B2 by a university language centre. The language centre has to be located at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.

These certificates may not be older than three years at the time of application.

Any of the above mentioned certificates at a higher level than B2, can also be accepted as proof of English language proficiency at level B2 ([see FAQ](#c805025)).

A proof of English language proficiency at level C1 is required for admission to the master programme in Anglophone Literatures and Cultures, the master programme in English Language and Linguistics and English (Teaching Subject) in the master programme Teacher Education.

[2] Proof of English language proficiency
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- English as first language ([see FAQ](#c805025)).
- Proof of erudite language: secondary-school leaving certificate, if the certificate was awarded at an institution
  - with English as the language of education
  - in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 110 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 (at least 7.5 per component)
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

A proof of English language proficiency at level C2 is required for the master programme in Translation, if one of the working languages is English.

[3] Proof of English language proficiency
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement

  - of a full-time degree programme of at least two years,
  - taught entirely in English,
  - at a university in an EU/EEA country, Switzerland, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, the USA, Australia, New Zealand or Canada.
- Degree diploma and transcript of records/diploma supplement
  - of a completed degree programme,
  - with English language specific courses,
  - to the extent of at least 36 ECTS.

These proofs are valid for an unlimited time.

- TOEFL: IBT (internet-based): 115 points or higher
- IELTS Academic: Overall Band Score: 8 or higher
- Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English (CAE): Grade A
- Cambridge Certificate of Proficiency in English (CPE): Grade C or higher

These proofs may not be older than three years at the time of application.

- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), especially §§ 63, 64](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)
- Decree: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/02_pdf/20250205-13.pdf) (05.02.2025)
- For applications for admission until winter semester 2024/25 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2021_2022/2021_2022_30.pdf) (17.12.2021)
- For applications for admission for the summer semester 2025 the following decree applies: [Decree by the Rectorate regarding the proof of English language proficiency required in the admission procedure](https://mtbl.univie.ac.at/storage/media/mtbl02/2024_2025/2024_2025_18.pdf) (23.10.2024)

Question: How many TOEFL points or what IELTS band do I need for an English-taught programme?

generate.raw_response: For most English-taught programmes, you need at least 94 TOEFL points [1] or an IELTS Overall Band Score of 6.5 [1]. However, some programmes may require higher scores, such as 110 TOEFL points [2] or an IELTS Overall Band Score of 8 [2]. It's best to check the specific requirements for your programme.
